Product Creation Code
Summary Guide
PHASE ONE: PRODUCT CREATION FOUNDATIONS
Lesson 2: The Magic Formula for Success
 Product creation success comes down to two things: Mindset and
Confidence
 Product creation is easier than ever before given the low-cost technology
at your disposal
 Don’t let those “dream stealers” or “naysayers” out there tell you that “You
Can’t Do It”
Lesson 3: What’s Your Perfect Day?
 Sit down and write down “Your Perfect Day”
o What would be the first thing you do?
o Who would you interact with?
o How would you go through your day?
o Where would you be living
o What would your home be like?
o What would your rituals be like?
 Write down at least two “BIG GOALS” you want to accomplish in your
business

-- 1 of 16 --

2
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Lesson 4: The Secrets to Creating a Killer Product
 Always remember these two secrets of success
o Build an email list
o Create products that the market wants
 Interact with the audience LOTS to find out what they want
 Go to targeted forums and social networks to gather information on
questions people have
 Run some paid advertising that includes a survey to gather more
information from your target audience
 Be careful not to fall in love with your idea…create what the market wants
 Remember that most people are beginners
 Fictional Guitar example:
o 5 million people interested in guitars
o 2.5 million will take the guitar out of the case
o 1.25 million will go beyond that first week to the beginner stage
o 50,000 competent guitar players
o 1,000 expert guitar players
 There are always more beginners then experts
 Remember…perfection is your enemy. Get your product out there!
 When you create a product, write your sales letter first!
o Forces you to create a great offer
o You won’t get caught up in creating perfect, but creating results
o You will get paid to bring value, results to people
o Need to build value into the pitch, which forces you to add value to
your product
 Make the shift from perfection mode to value creation mode

-- 2 of 16 --

3
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Lesson 5: How to “Move the Free Line”
 Remember Eben Pagan’s concept, “Move the Free Line”
 As you move the free line, giving massive value and awesome free
content, then your price will increase and you can charge higher prices
 Jeff never wants to compete on price…don’t be an “Online Walmart”.
 Two ways to charge more:
o Move the free line
o Create tremendous value in your products
 It is much easier to sell 1,000 people a $1,000 product then 1,000,000
people a $1 product
 You want to sell on value NOT on price
 It is inevitable that the ones that pay more for your high-priced product will
actually be opening it up and using it
 Deliver value at all costs
Lesson 6: Know Your Math
 Internet businesses sometimes employ an “Arbitrage Play”:
o Buy something in one market for one price and sell it in another
market for another price
 Example
o One product – 10% refund rate on $1,000 ($900 net)
o Another product – 50% refund rate on a $10,000 product ($5,000
net)
 Conversion rates vary significantly depending on price point and market
Lesson 7: Perception is Everything!
 Value perception is critical to the success of your products
 If you have discounts and sales all the time, then you have killed the
perceived value…people will just wait for the discounts

-- 3 of 16 --

4
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
 Changing the format of a product can change the value perception
 Example Book
o “River Rescue Book” - $20
o Workshop (similar information) - $200-$500
o Laminated Cards - $20 (huge value)
 Make your products FUNGIBLE
o Take that information and mold/re-shape to appeal to different
people
o Different formats
o Radically changes your results
 See a perfect example of this in the two video case examples provided
with this lesson
PHASE TWO: PRODUCT CREATION STRATEGY
Lesson 8: How to Select Your Niche
 Key questions to consider:
o What should my product be about?
o What should I publish about?
o What do I know that I can share with people?
o What have you been doing for five years or more?
o What have you been working on?
o What is your passion area?
o What do people ask you about?
o What comes super easy to you?
 Different ways to find your niche

-- 4 of 16 --

5
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Follow your Bliss (Joseph Campbell) – do what you love
o Be a Mercenary – find a good market and publish yourself or find
an expert to publish
 Test the Market
o Are people selling in these markets?
o If there is NO competition, then be very careful
o Find a market and be successful in and be better
o Find a market that people are passionate about
Lesson 9: Secrets to Finding a Rabid Market
 People often will buy one course after another and never go through it or
just review it a bit
 Buying a product makes your prospect feel like they have accomplished
something (the psychology of people buying a lot of information)
 If you have a rabid market, you will find information junkies who will buy
your product and may not even use it
Lesson 10: “Be the Wizard, Beware the Wizard”
 Don’t “Fake it until you make it” (this can adversely affect people’s lives)
 Dan Kennedy popular, saying “Be the wizard, beware the wizard”
o You are becoming the “wizard” in your market
o OR you are the marketer bringing the “wizard” to your market
 Beware the wizard – be careful of who you are learning from
Lesson 11: Identifying Your Unique Connection Point
 Find a “Unique Connection Point” with your market

-- 5 of 16 --

6
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Discover a way to connect with your market and show your
personality and who you are
o People don’t like to buy from corporations…they like to buy from
people they “know-like-trust”
 Create a connection point by sharing your experience, personality and
stories
 People commonly remember stories or a teaching point that was
conveyed through a story
 Stories keep your audience interested and engaged
 Be personable and human
 You can sell a lot more from a “personality-based” business
Lesson 12: Finding Experts
 You don’t need to be the expert…you can be the “marketing expert”
 Find the expert and partner up
o Experts are a dime a dozen regardless of the topic
 Think of a hobby and do a search on Amazon.com
o Books written by experts
 Things to remember when partnering with an expert:
o Establish written agreement with the expert
 Write it down so you clearly know what you are both
agreeing to
o Maintain ownership at all costs (51% at least)
 Ideally 70/30 split
o Experts must be exceptional
 Awesome skills
 Great person
 Select someone who values you for your marketing skills
o Remember, you bring more value than the expert

-- 6 of 16 --

7
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Lesson 13: Product Creation Mindset
 Two ways to create a product
o Your expertise
o Outside expertise
 Your expertise
o What are you passionate about?
o What do people ask you about?
o What subject is really easy for you?
o What’s always on your mind?
 Outside expertise
o Find experts on blogs
o Find experts on forums
o Find experts through Amazon (published books)
o Marketplace (guru, elance)
o Expertise is all around waiting to be captured
 Identify hot questions in your market
o What are the questions that keep showing up in the forums?
o How do you answer these questions?
o What are the “basics” that everyone must know?
o What are the “Ninja tricks” that you can teach?
 Look at the possible market gaps
o What is the biggest gap in your marketplace?
o What is not being covered by other products?
o What are the best magazines and top articles that keep coming up?
o What are the best books?
 Key takeaways
o You’ve got more expertise then you think you do
o Avoid the “Curse of Knowledge” – hard to remember what it was
like to be a beginner
o You can get help with your product…there are lots of experts out
there
o Selling “Basics” is always a solid business model…lots of
beginners in every market
o Don’t be scared by lots of competition in your market…lots of
competition shows it is a healthy marketplace

-- 7 of 16 --

8
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
Lesson 14: Product Creation Foundation
 Identify your purpose(s) for creating your product
o Make money
o Build a list of clients
o Drive traffic
o Build a list of partners
o Build positioning
 There is certainly lots of overlap in these areas
 Determine if this is a product your market wants
o What are people saying in the forums
 Example: Google “Learn Guitar Forums”
o Are advertisers spending money
o Is there some competition
 Identify your price point
o Need to test the product to find the pricing “sweet spot”
o Review competitor pricing and determine what the market will pay
 Look at the most expensive products and the cheapest ones
to see where your product might fit
 If you create more value and have better marketing, it allows
you to be at the high end
 Determine your production cost
o What does it cost to produce the product?
o Does it give you enough margin?
Lesson 15: Product Creation vs Affiliate Marketing
Affiliate Marketing
 PROS
o Simple and easy to get started
o No product creation costs
o Able to focus just on traffic generation
o No support costs
o Simple technology
 CONS
o Zero control of sales funnel
o Inability to manage customer relationships
o Hard to set-up your own offers and upsells
o Hard to build a list

-- 8 of 16 --

9
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Less then 100% of the profit
Product Creation
 PROS
o Control over the whole process
o Keep 100% of the profits
o Easier to build relationship with prospects and buyers
 Someone on client list is worth 15 times more than a
prospect list
o Easier to build list
o Great data on what works in the market
o Ability to create a brand new niche or sub-niche
o Ability to get affiliates working for you
 CONS
o Customer support issues and maintenance
o Higher risk due to uncertainty of success in the market
o More technical complexities in the process
o More time, energy and cost
 Remember, affiliate marketing can be a money maker, but it is not a
business
 Easy to start in affiliate marketing, but look to create your own products as
soon as possible
 One good strategy is to partner with your competitors and promote each
others products
Lesson 16: Product Idea Factory
 Places to source product ideas
o Google Trends
o Clickbank
o Amazon
o Magazines
o Blogs
o Targeted forums
o Social media
o Word of mouth
o Yahoo Answers
 Fast Product Formats

10

All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Use audio
o Use video
 Screen capture
 Full motion
o Write content
 Work off of an outline
 Use “Hot Pen Technique” - Start typing and DON’T let
yourself stop typing for a specific time period
o Bundle products together
 Combine similar products together (possibly products you
have the right to re-sell)
 Transform content
 Transcribe audio
 Turn ebook into video series
 Video to audio MP3s
 Build big value by reaching different types of people
o Speed Matters
 Momentum creates momentum
 Don’t sit around too long thinking about your product
 Breeds procrastination and failure
 Get the first version of your product out there
 Gives you the ability to test multiple markets and pricing
 Faster feedback from the market
 Don’t put all your eggs in one “product” basket
PHASE THREE: PRODUCT CREATION FORMATS AND TOOLS
Lesson 17: Product Storyteller
 Always look to add stories in your products or turn your product into a
story (very powerful)
 Four roles to play in product creation
o Interviewer
 Gather experts and interview them on the same or different
topics
o Expert
 Teacher or guru
o Researcher
 Research market, gather data and convert to a product

-- 10 of 16 --

11
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Bundler
 Bundle together other products that you have private-label
rights (re-sell rights)
 Bundle several products and add in your own stuff
Lesson 18: Product Formats, Part One
 Audio product
o Simplest product to create
o Inexpensive
o Ideal for interview-style products
 Video product
o Hot medium right now
o Lot more impactful than audio
o People can tangibly see your personality
o Can strip out audio and create both video and audio products
o Offer online delivery
o Can create DVDs
o Two types of videos to create
 Full Motion (shot with a camera)
 Screen capture (great for demos, powerpoint, mindmaps,
etc…)
 Editing with screen capture is easier
o Always make sure your audio is really good
 Ebooks (PDFs)
o Write one quickly
o Ebook delivery growing rapidly on iPad and Amazon Kindle
o Types:
 Reports
 Consumer awareness guide
 Hot tips
 Cheat sheets
 Manuals
 Checklists
 Action guides
 How to
 Reference material
 Physical books
o Low perceived value
o Trade published or self-published

-- 11 of 16 --

12
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
o Trade Published
 Give up lots of control
 Rarely make a lot of money
 Still have to do the marketing
 Can do a lot for your positioning
o Self-published
 You have all the control
 You might make some money
 You have to do all the marketing
 Not as much “positioning power” as trade published books
Lesson 19: Product Formats, Part Two
 In person: 1-to-1 or 1-to-many
o Coaching or consulting
 Works well if higher priced
 Webinars
o One off event
o Powerpoint style or live streaming video
o Can sell them as a paid event
o Can be part of a coaching series (3-10 webinars)
 Example: Product Launch Formula was first offered as a
series of teleseminars
o Can be interactive (take questions)
o Offer a replay
o Recording can become a product
 Teleseminars
o Only audio (bridge line)
o Types
 Interviews
 Coaching
 How To
 Reviews
o Best practices
 Record call and get it transcribed
 Get real questions from the audience
 What to give audience
 Number and pin number
 Date and time

-- 12 of 16 --

13
© 2012 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
All Rights Reserved.
v.1
 Tweet about it
 Send email reminders (starting soon, starting now)
 Clarify time zone
 Let them send questions ahead of time
 Never be late
 Live events
o Expensive to put on
o Can be complicated
o Can be risky
 Room guarantees
o But can be a great way to create a product
 Can record audio and video
 Likely need serious preparation and editing
Lesson 20: Product Formats, Part Three
 Continuity programs
o People pay you ongoing monthly fee
o Membership sites
o Training course (monthly training product)
o Newsletter (physical or enewsletter)
o CDs or DVD of the month
 Benefit of continuity program is that you keep getting paid
 Main challenge is the need to keep adding new people
o Your renewal rate will be your most important metric
 Home study courses
o Not as common these days as products are more digital
 Coaching programs
o Group calls or webinars
o Download guides / PDFs
Lesson 21: Product Creation Tools, Part One
 Webinars
o GoToWebinar.com
 Teleseminars
o Search for “Bridge Lines” in Google
